title: aspidotis meifolia
intro: Aspidotis meifolia, also known as the western cliff brake, is a type of flowering plant in the fern family (Aspidiaceae). Traditionally, Aspidotis meifolia has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been valued for its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as its potential to help alleviate respiratory issues and coughs. Aspidotis meifolia can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
